para.name: purpose
para.heading: PURPOSE
para.text: To give management an opportunity to review and ensure the quality and completeness of all grant applications and supporting documentation, e.g., technical and cost proposals, prior to submission.

para.name: scope
para.heading: SCOPE
para.text: This policy is applicable to all city of Baltimore agencies and/or entities that use grant funding, as well as any other organizations for which the city serves as grantor or contributes resources. Moreover, this policy supersedes and replaces AM-404-1 Financial Grants, AM-404-1-1 Applying for Grants, and AM-404-1-2 Action Upon Receiving Grant Approval.

para.name: policy-statement
para.heading: POLICY STATEMENT
para.text: An agency/entity's senior management must review and approve all grant applications to ensure the quality and completeness of each application - at a reasonable or specified price - prior to submission.

para.name: procedure
para.heading: PROCEDURE
para.text: Grant Proposal Manager:
Forwards the draft grant application and budget to agency's internal grants management committee (GMC) for review and approval.
Grants Management Committee (GMC):
Ensures grant application meets the requirements detailed in the NOFA or RFP Scope of Work or as designated by key stakeholders, prior to the agency's fiscal officer review; and,
If changes are required, returns draft document(s) with changes noted to the grant proposal manager for revision.
Agency/Entity's Fiscal Officer and/or Budget Proposal Manager:
In collaboration with the agency/entity's BBMR Budget Analyst, ensures grant application meets the requirements detailed in the Scope of Work prior to senior management's review; and,
If necessary, returns draft document(s) to budget proposal manager for correction or changes.
Chairperson Grants Management Committee:
Ensures grant application meets the requirements detailed in the Scope of Work and approves for submission;
If necessary, returns draft document(s) to the appropriate staff member for correction(s); and,
Records the name of the GMC approver and the date the grant application was approved on the CRM site, at: at https://bmore.crm9.dynamics.com/main.aspx.
Grant Proposal Manager:
Upon receipt of the approved application, delivers/submits grant as detailed in the Notice of Funding Availability (NOFA) or Request for Proposal (RFP);
Forwards a copy of the completed grant application to the grants management office and archives a copy, with all supporting documentation, in SharePoint.

para.name: policy-owner
para.heading: POLICY OWNER
para.text: The grants management office is responsible for all changes and/or updates to this policy.

para.name: flow-process
para.heading: FLOW PROCESS
para.text: Figure 1, below, displays the primary steps followed in the grant review process.
Figure 1: Grant Management Review gives management an opportunity to review all grant submissions to ensure the pre-submission quality of the grant application.
